title: Local Business AI Operations Kit
subtitle: The Practical AI Playbook for Brick-and-Mortar Businesses
tagline: Your competitor down the street is already using AI. Here's how to use it better.

sections.title: The Local Business AI Audit
sections.subtitle: Know exactly where AI will save you time and money before spending a single dollar
sections.content:
Most local business owners hear 'AI' and picture robots or Silicon Valley startups. Here's the reality: AI for your business is just software that handles repetitive tasks faster than a human can, without taking a lunch break or calling in sick. You already have tasks that qualify. This audit will show you exactly which ones.

## The 20 Tasks Every Local Business Does That AI Can Handle Better

Work through this list. Put a checkmark next to every task you or your staff currently do manually:

1. Responding to Google Business messages and inquiries
2. Answering frequently asked questions (hours, pricing, location, parking)
3. Sending appointment reminders to clients
4. Following up with customers who haven't returned in 60+ days
5. Requesting reviews after a completed service
6. Responding to online reviews (Google, Yelp, Facebook)
7. Writing social media captions for posts
8. Creating monthly email newsletters
9. Drafting promotional offers and sale announcements
10. Writing job postings when hiring
11. Creating staff training documents and SOPs
12. Generating quotes or estimates from a template
13. Sending invoices and payment reminders
14. Answering DMs on Instagram or Facebook
15. Writing descriptions for services or products
16. Compiling customer feedback into a summary
17. Creating weekly or monthly performance reports
18. Responding to complaints in writing
19. Writing responses to Yelp or TripAdvisor inquiries
20. Drafting supplier or vendor emails

Count your checkmarks. If you checked 10 or more, you are leaving significant time and money on the table every single week.

## The Priority Matrix: Where to Start

Not all tasks are equal. Use this framework to decide what to automate first:

**High Impact / Low Effort — Do These First (Week 1-2)**
- Review response automation: Takes 30 minutes to set up, saves 3-5 hours per month, and visibly improves your online reputation immediately.
- FAQ chatbot or auto-reply: Set up once on Google Business Profile and Facebook. Answers 80% of routine questions without you touching anything.
- Appointment reminder texts: Reduces no-shows by 30-50% at most businesses. Pays for itself in the first week.
- Post-service review requests: Most businesses see a 40-80% increase in review volume within 30 days of automating this.

**High Impact / Medium Effort — Do These in Week 3-4**
- Social media content system: Takes 2-3 hours to build a content template and prompt library. Then cuts your weekly social media time from 3 hours to 30 minutes.
- Email marketing sequences: Build a 3-email welcome sequence and a monthly newsletter template. This runs automatically once built.
- Re-engagement campaigns: Set up a trigger that sends a message to any customer who hasn't visited in 60 days. Most businesses recover 5-15% of lapsed customers this way.

**Medium Impact / Low Effort — Fill In After the Above**
- Staff training document generation
- Quote and estimate templates
- Seasonal promotion copy

## The 30-Day Implementation Calendar

**Days 1-3: Foundation**
- Sign up for ChatGPT Plus ($20/month) or Claude Pro ($20/month). You need one AI writing assistant. Either works.
- Audit your Google Business Profile. Ensure your hours, phone number, and address are correct. Enable messaging.
- List every FAQ you get asked weekly. You will turn these into auto-responses.

**Days 4-7: Quick Wins**
- Build your review response template library (covered in Section 3).
- Set up appointment reminder automation through your booking software (most have this built in — you just need to turn it on and write the message).
- Create your first AI-generated social media content batch: 2 weeks of posts in 1 sitting.

**Days 8-14: Communication Automation**
- Set up auto-responses for Google Business Messages.
- Build your post-service review request sequence.
- Create a re-engagement email for customers who haven't visited in 60 days.

**Days 15-21: Content and Marketing**
- Build your email newsletter template.
- Generate a month of social media content.
- Optimize your Google Business Profile description using AI (covered in Section 4).

**Days 22-30: Operations**
- Document your top 3 staff training procedures using AI.
- Build your complaint-response framework.
- Review what's working and double down.

## How to Explain AI to Your Staff Without Creating Fear

This is the conversation most business owners avoid and shouldn't. Here is exactly what to say:

'We are adding some tools that handle the repetitive communication stuff — like reminders, review responses, and answering basic questions. This isn't replacing anyone. It means you spend less time on admin and more time with actual customers. Your job doesn't change. You just get some backup on the boring stuff.'

That's it. The fear of AI in workplaces comes from vague language. Be specific about what it does (answers texts, writes reminders) and what it does not do (replace the human judgment, creativity, and relationship-building that your staff provides).

Practical tips for staff rollout:
- Show them the tool before it goes live. Let them try it.
- Make clear that AI drafts; humans review and approve (especially for complaints).
- Ask for their input on what phrases sound 'off' and refine templates together.
- Frame it as a time-saver, because that is what it is.

## The ROI Calculation: What AI Is Actually Worth to Your Business

Use these formulas to calculate your specific return:

**Time Saved Formula**
Hours per week on admin tasks x your effective hourly rate = weekly cost of manual operations

Example: A salon owner spends 8 hours per week on booking follow-ups, review responses, and social media. At an owner's time value of $75/hour, that's $600/week or $31,200/year in owner time.

AI tool cost: $20-100/month = $240-1,200/year
Time saved: 6 of those 8 hours per week
Annual savings: ~$23,400 in recaptured owner time

**No-Show Reduction Formula**
Monthly no-shows x average ticket value x 0.35 (estimated recovery rate with reminders) = monthly revenue recovered

Example: A gym with 40 personal training no-shows per month at $65/session x 0.35 = $910/month recovered just from reminder automation.

**Review Impact Formula**
Each additional 0.1-star increase in Google rating = approximately 5-9% increase in clicks to your business listing (Google's own data).

Going from 4.1 stars to 4.4 stars, which typically happens within 90 days of review automation, can mean 15-27% more discovery traffic from Google — without spending anything on ads.

**Lapsed Customer Recovery Formula**
Lapsed customers in your database x 10% recovery rate x average annual customer value = revenue from re-engagement campaign

Example: 300 lapsed customers x 10% x $400/year average spend = $12,000 in recovered annual revenue from one email campaign.

Add these three figures together for your business. Most local businesses land between $15,000 and $60,000 in annual value from basic AI implementation — against a tool cost of under $150/month.

sections.title: Customer Communication Automation
sections.subtitle: How to be everywhere your customers reach out — without being available 24/7
sections.content:
The average local business misses 30-40% of inbound inquiries. Not because owners don't care — because they are busy running the operation. A customer texts at 9pm asking if you're open Saturday. Nobody responds until Monday morning. By then, they've booked with a competitor. Communication automation fixes this problem permanently.

## The Inquiry Response Stack

Customers reach out through five main channels. Here is what to set up on each:

**1. Google Business Profile Messages**
This is your highest-priority channel. Google actively promotes businesses that respond quickly.

Setup: Open Google Business Profile Manager > Messages > Turn on. Then set your Welcome Message.

Welcome message template (customize this):
'Hi! Thanks for reaching out to [Business Name]. We typically reply within a few hours. If you need immediate help, call us at [phone]. To book an appointment, visit [booking link]. For hours and location, visit [website].'

For auto-FAQ responses, use the Saved Replies feature. Create replies for:
- Hours (include holiday hours)
- Parking and location
- Pricing ranges
- How to book
- What to expect on first visit

Advanced: Connect Google Business Messages to a tool like Podium, Birdeye, or even a Zapier automation that routes messages to your SMS so you can reply from your phone without being in the dashboard.

**2. Facebook and Instagram DMs (Meta Business Suite)**
Meta Business Suite (free at business.facebook.com) has built-in auto-reply and away message features that most local businesses never use.

Setup: Meta Business Suite > Inbox > Automations

Create three automations:
1. Instant Reply: Fires immediately when someone sends a first message. 'Thanks for your message! We'll get back to you within [X hours]. For faster service, call [phone] or book at [link].'
2. Away Message: Active during closed hours. Same format, but acknowledge you'll reply next business day.
3. FAQ Quick Replies: Create 5-10 buttons that answer your most common questions. Customers tap a button instead of waiting for a reply.

For businesses getting 20+ DMs per week: Add an AI layer. Connect your Instagram/Facebook inbox to ManyChat (starting at $15/month). ManyChat lets you build conversation flows that can qualify leads, capture contact information, and route to a human when needed.

**3. SMS and Phone Texts**
If you are not texting with customers, you are behind. 98% of texts are opened; 20% of emails are.

Tools to enable business texting:
- Google Voice (free, basic)
- OpenPhone ($13/month per user) — best for small teams
- Podium ($249/month, full platform) — best for volume businesses
- Birdeye ($299/month) — best for multi-location or reputation-heavy businesses

Key automations to build in your texting platform:
- Appointment confirmation: Sends immediately upon booking
- 48-hour reminder: 'Just a reminder: [service] with [staff name] on [date] at [time]. Reply C to confirm or call [phone] to reschedule.'
- 2-hour reminder: Day-of reminder for high no-show businesses
- Post-service follow-up: Sends 24 hours after appointment (covered below)

**4. Email Inquiries**
For email inquiries from your website contact form, set up an autoresponder in Gmail or your email client:

Subject line: 'Got your message — here's what happens next'

Body: 'Hi [Name], thanks for reaching out to [Business Name]. We received your message and will follow up within [X hours/1 business day]. In the meantime: [Link to book online / FAQ page / pricing page]. Talk soon, [Your name]'

For businesses using a CRM (HubSpot Free, Zoho, etc.), set up a contact form trigger that automatically creates a follow-up task and sends this email.

**5. Yelp Messaging**
Often overlooked. Yelp Business (free) has an inbox with auto-reply functionality. Enable it and set a message similar to your Google auto-reply.

## Booking and Appointment Management

If you are still taking bookings only by phone, you are losing customers daily. 40% of bookings happen outside business hours.

**Booking Tools by Business Type:**
- Restaurants: OpenTable, Resy, or the Google Reservations integration (free)
- Salons/Spas: Vagaro ($25/month), Square Appointments (free-$69/month), Fresha (free)
- Gyms: Mindbody ($129/month), Glofox ($110/month), or Pike13
- Service businesses: Calendly ($10/month), Acuity Scheduling ($16/month)
- General: Google Booking (free, integrates directly with GBP)

Once your booking tool is set up, connect it to:
1. Your Google Business Profile (Book Now button)
2. Your Instagram bio link
3. Your website header
4. Every email signature

## Follow-Up Sequences After Appointments

This is the single highest-ROI automation most local businesses are not running. The sequence:

**Message 1 — 24 hours after service:**
'Hi [Name], hope you're loving your [service]! If you have any questions or need anything, just reply here. We'd love to see you again — you can rebook anytime at [link].'

**Message 2 — 3 days after service (only if no response to Message 1):**
'Hi [Name], just checking in from [Business Name]. Your next [service] is typically due around [date estimate]. Book your spot before we fill up: [link]. Thanks for being a great client!'

**Message 3 — Review request (day 5, only if no negative signal):**
Covered in Section 3.

Build these in your texting platform or email tool using time-delay automations. Total build time: 45 minutes. Estimated impact: 15-25% increase in rebooking rate.

## Handling Complaints with AI + Human Oversight

Never fully automate complaint responses. The process that works:

1. AI draft: Use this prompt in ChatGPT: 'Write a professional, empathetic response to this customer complaint: [paste complaint]. The response should: acknowledge the issue without admitting fault, express genuine concern, offer a specific resolution, and invite them to contact us directly to resolve. Keep it under 150 words and do not use corporate-sounding language.'

2. Human review: Read the AI draft. Check that the resolution offer matches your actual policy. Add any specific detail that the AI wouldn't know (e.g., 'I looked up your order and...').

3. Personalize: Change any phrase that sounds generic. Add the customer's name. Sign it personally.

Rule of thumb: AI handles 80% of the writing. You handle 100% of the decision about what to offer.

## Specific Tool Recommendations with Pricing

| Tool | Best For | Monthly Cost | What It Does |
|------|----------|--------------|---------------|
| Podium | High-volume service businesses | $249 | Texts, reviews, payments, inbox |
| Birdeye | Reputation-focused businesses | $299 | Reviews, messaging, surveys, AI responses |
| OpenPhone | Small teams needing texting | $13/user | Business phone with texting |
| ManyChat | Instagram/Facebook DM automation | $15-65 | Automated DM flows |
| Acuity Scheduling | Service-based booking | $16 | Online booking with reminders |
| Vagaro | Salons and spas | $25 | Booking, marketing, payments |
| Zapier | Connecting everything | $20-50 | Automates connections between tools |

For a budget-conscious business just starting: Google Business Messages (free) + OpenPhone ($13) + your existing booking tool's reminders = 80% of the value at under $30/month.

sections.title: Review Management and Reputation AI
sections.subtitle: Your Google rating is worth more than any ad you'll ever run
sections.content:
Here is a number that should get your attention: 93% of consumers say online reviews impact their purchase decisions. For local businesses, your Google rating is the first thing a potential customer sees when they search your business name. A business with 4.6 stars and 200 reviews will consistently outperform a better business with 4.1 stars and 40 reviews — in clicks, calls, and foot traffic. This section is about engineering that outcome systematically.

## Why Reviews Are the Lifeblood of Local Business

Google's algorithm for Local Pack ranking (the three businesses that show up in map results) heavily weights:
- Review quantity (more is better)
- Review recency (fresh reviews outperform old ones)
- Review rating
- Whether you respond to reviews

This means a consistent flow of new reviews isn't just about reputation — it directly impacts how many people find your business in the first place. A business that generates 10 new reviews per month will rank higher six months from now than the same business generating 2 reviews per month, everything else equal.

## Automated Review Monitoring

Step 1: Set up alerts so you never miss a review.

**Google Reviews:** In Google Business Profile, ensure email notifications are enabled for new reviews. Settings > Notifications > New reviews.

**Yelp:** Yelp Business app notifies you of new reviews automatically. Enable push notifications.

**Facebook:** Meta Business Suite > Notifications > Reviews.

**All-in-one monitoring (recommended):** Google Alerts is free but limited. For serious monitoring, use:
- ReviewTrackers (starts at $45/month) — aggregates all review platforms
- Birdeye — reviews plus response tools
- Grade.us — good for agencies and multi-location businesses
- Free option: Set a Google Alert for '[Your Business Name] review' — catches blog posts and some platform reviews

Target: See every new review within 4 hours of it being posted.

## AI-Generated Response Templates That Sound Human

The goal is not to sound like a bot. The goal is to sound like a business owner who genuinely cares but doesn't have time to write a novel for every review. Here is how to build a response template library:

**The Master Prompt for 5-Star Responses:**
'Write 5 different responses to this 5-star Google review for [Business Type]. The responses should: be warm and personal (not corporate), vary in length from 2-4 sentences, avoid using the same phrases, thank the specific person by name, mention one specific thing from their review, and end with an invitation to return. Vary the opening so they don't all start with 'Thank you.' Review: [paste review]'

Generate 5 versions. Save the two best. Use them alternately.

**Template Library to Build (5-star reviews):**
Create 10 templates of varying length and tone. Rotate through them. The variation makes responses look genuinely written, not templated.

Example template set:
- Short and warm: 'So glad you had a great experience, [Name]! [Specific comment reference]. This is exactly why we do what we do. See you next time!'
- Medium with detail: '[Name], thank you for taking the time to share this — we loved having you in. [Specific reference to their review.] [Staff name] will be especially happy to hear this. We look forward to seeing you again.'
- Inviting: 'What a great review, [Name]! [Reference specific element.] We're always working to make sure every visit feels like this one. Next time, ask about [relevant offer/service] — we think you'll love it too.'

**For 4-Star Reviews (someone liked it but didn't love one thing):**
Prompt: 'Write a response to this 4-star review that: thanks them genuinely, addresses the concern mentioned without being defensive, explains what you're doing about it (or invites them to share more), and ends positively. Keep it under 100 words. Review: [paste review]'

**For Negative Reviews (1-3 stars):**
Prompt: 'Write a professional, non-defensive response to this negative review for a [business type]. Acknowledge the experience, apologize for falling short (without admitting specific fault), offer a direct line to resolve it, and keep the tone calm and genuine. Do not offer compensation publicly. Under 100 words. Review: [paste review]'

Critical rule for negative reviews: Always move the conversation offline. Include a phone number or email in every negative response: 'Please reach out directly to [name] at [contact] and we'll make this right.'

## The Review Generation Playbook

The ethical and effective way to get more 5-star reviews:

**The Golden Moment Technique**
Ask for a review at the exact moment the customer expresses satisfaction. This is called the golden moment. Examples:
- Customer says 'I love it!' after a haircut — that is the moment. 'We're so glad! Would you mind leaving us a quick Google review? It makes a huge difference for us.' Hand them a QR code card.
- Customer says 'That was the best meal I've had in a while' — same script, same QR code.

**Automated Post-Service Review Request (Text)**
Send this 24-48 hours after service:
'Hi [Name], this is [Staff/Owner] from [Business]. Hope you're enjoying your [service/product]! If you have a minute, a Google review would mean the world to us: [short link]. Takes 30 seconds. Thank you!'

To get a short review link: Google Business Profile > Get more reviews > Copy the link. Shorten it at bit.ly or use a QR code generator.

**The QR Code Card**
Print a small card (business card size) with your Google review QR code. Place them:
- At the checkout counter
- In the packaging when applicable
- On tables (restaurants)
- In the follow-up text

Estimated cost: $15-30 at Vistaprint or Canva + local printer.

**Email Review Request**
For businesses with email lists, send a dedicated review request 2-3 days after service. Subject line: 'Quick favor? (Takes 30 seconds)'

Body: 'Hi [Name], quick note — we loved having you in [or: we hope you enjoyed your [service/product]]. If you have 30 seconds, leaving us a Google review would genuinely help our small business. It means more than you know. [Big button: Leave a Google Review → link]'

**What NOT to Do:**
- Do not offer discounts or gifts in exchange for reviews. This violates Google's terms and Yelp's policies and can get your listing penalized.
- Do not ask customers to review while they are in your business on your device. Google flags this.
- Do not pay for reviews. Ever.

## Crisis Response: Negative Review Handling Protocol

A negative review is not a crisis. A negative review handled badly is a crisis. Here is the exact protocol:

1. Breathe first. Do not respond when angry. Set a 2-hour minimum before responding to anything below 3 stars.
2. Investigate before responding. Pull up the transaction. Know the facts.
3. Use the AI prompt above to generate a response draft.
4. Personalize it. Add any relevant context (without being defensive).
5. Post the response and move on. Do not reply again if they escalate. Silence after your professional response is powerful.
6. Follow up offline if they give contact info.

For fake or malicious reviews: Flag the review in Google. Document why it's fake. If the review is from a non-customer or contains false information, Google will often remove it within 7-14 days when properly flagged.

## Competitive Review Monitoring

Know what your competitors' customers are complaining about. This is free market research.

Monthly habit (20 minutes): Read the 1-2 star reviews for your top 3 competitors. Common complaints become your marketing opportunities.

Example: If three competitors all have 2-star reviews mentioning 'long wait times,' your marketing message should be 'In and out in under [X] minutes, guaranteed' (if that's true for you).

Track this in a simple Google Sheet: Competitor name | Common complaint | Our advantage | Where we mention it

sections.title: Marketing Automation Without an Agency
sections.subtitle: How to produce agency-quality content in a fraction of the time and cost
sections.content:
The average local business pays $1,500-5,000/month to a marketing agency for services that can largely be replicated with AI tools and 3-4 hours per week of your time. This section doesn't replace a great agency — but it gives you independence, quality control, and the ability to move faster than any agency can.

## AI-Powered Social Media Content

The framework that works: Batch creation + templates + scheduling.

**The Content Batching System**
Set aside 2 hours every two weeks. In those 2 hours, create all your social content for the next 14 days using this system:

Step 1 — Choose your content mix (the 4-1-1 rule for local business):
- 4 posts: Educational or entertaining (tips, behind-the-scenes, staff features, community involvement)
- 1 post: Promotional (offer, sale, new service)
- 1 post: Social proof (review highlight, before/after, customer story)

Step 2 — Use this master prompt to generate captions in batch:
'I run a [business type] called [business name] in [city]. My audience is [describe customer — e.g., local families, professionals aged 25-45, fitness-minded people]. Generate 6 social media captions for the following content plan: [list your 6 topics, one per line]. For each caption: write in a conversational, non-salesy tone; include a question or call to action; keep it under 150 words; and vary the format (some start with a question, some with a bold statement, some with a story opener). Do not use hashtags yet — I'll add those separately.'

Step 3 — Add hashtags with a second prompt:
'Generate 10-15 relevant hashtags for a [business type] in [city]. Mix of: 3 broad industry tags, 3 local community tags, 3 niche-specific tags, and 2-3 branded or unique tags. Format as a ready-to-paste block.'

Step 4 — Schedule using:
- Meta Business Suite (free) for Facebook and Instagram
- Google Business Profile posts (free, for your GBP feed)
- Later.com ($18/month) for multi-platform scheduling with a visual calendar
- Buffer ($6/month) for simple scheduling

**What Actually Performs for Local Businesses:**

Based on engagement patterns across local business types:
- Video beats photos 3-to-1. Even a 15-second clip shot on your phone of someone doing their job outperforms a polished graphic post.
- Faces outperform logos. Posts with staff or customers in them get 2-3x the engagement of product-only or graphic posts.
- Local tags and location mentions consistently increase reach.
- 'Behind the scenes' content (showing how something is made or done) drives high save rates, which signals to the algorithm.
- Posts that ask a question get more comments, which the algorithm rewards.

**Photo and Video AI Tools:**
- Canva AI (free tier available): Generate graphics, remove backgrounds, create templates
- CapCut (free): Edit Reels and TikToks with AI captions and effects on your phone
- Descript ($12/month): Record and edit talking-head or tutorial videos with AI cleanup

## Email Marketing for Local Businesses

The businesses that do email marketing consistently outperform those that don't. The typical local business list is worth $5-15 per subscriber per year in revenue.

**Building Your List:**
- Add a sign-up form to your website footer
- Offer a simple incentive: '10% off your next visit when you join our list'
- Use a tablet at checkout with a sign-up form (Mailchimp and Klaviyo both have mobile signup forms)
- Add your signup link to every post-service follow-up text

**Tools:**
- Mailchimp (free up to 500 contacts, $13/month after): Best for beginners
- Klaviyo ($20/month): Better automation and segmentation
- Constant Contact ($12/month): Simple and reliable

**What to Send and How Often:**

Frequency: Once per week maximum. Once per month minimum. Twice per month is the sweet spot for most local businesses.

The 4 email types that work:
1. Monthly newsletter: Community news, a tip, a highlight. Not a sales pitch. Builds trust. AI prompt: 'Write a 200-word monthly newsletter for a [business type] in [city]. Include: one helpful tip related to [industry], a brief mention of [event/update at business], and a soft call to action. Friendly, local-feeling tone.'
2. Seasonal promotion: 'Spring special' or 'Holiday gift cards.' Clear offer, clear deadline, clear button.
3. New service or product announcement: Tell them what's new, why it's for them, how to get it.
4. Re-engagement: Targets customers who haven't opened in 90 days. Subject line: 'We miss you — here's something for you.' Includes a specific offer.

**Subject Lines That Get Opened (Local Business Version):**
- '[First name], something new at [Business]'
- 'Our biggest week of the year is coming up'
- 'Quick question for you, [First name]'
- 'The [service] secret we've been keeping'
- 'Last chance: [Specific offer] ends [day]'

Generate 5 subject line options with AI and A/B test them. Mailchimp and Klaviyo both support subject line A/B testing.

## Google Business Profile Optimization with AI

Your GBP listing is free real estate that most businesses waste. A fully optimized listing:
- Ranks higher in local search
- Shows more information before a customer even clicks
- Builds trust before the first contact

**The GBP AI Optimization Audit — Do This Today:**

1. Business Description: Should be 750 characters. Use this prompt: 'Write a 750-character Google Business Profile description for [Business Name], a [business type] in [city/neighborhood]. Include: what we specialize in, who we serve, what makes us different, and a call to action. Use natural language (not keyword stuffing). Weave in these service terms naturally: [list 3-5 services].' Update it in GBP under 'Edit Profile.'

2. Products/Services section: Add every individual service with a name, description, and price range. Use AI to write 3-sentence descriptions for each. This dramatically increases visibility for specific service searches.

3. Q&A section: You can post your own questions and answers. Seed it with your top 10 FAQs. AI prompt: 'Write 10 FAQ questions and answers for a [business type]. Cover: pricing, hours, booking process, what to expect on first visit, parking, cancellation policy, payment methods, and what differentiates the business. Keep answers under 100 words each.'

4. GBP Posts: Post to GBP at least once per week. GBP posts appear in search results and have a 7-day lifespan. Schedule them using your social media calendar.

## Local SEO Content Generation

For businesses with a website, regularly adding location-specific content improves local search rankings. AI makes this fast.

**The Local Content Formula:**
Create a simple blog section on your website (WordPress, Squarespace, and Wix all support this). Publish one 600-800 word post per month using this formula:

Prompt: 'Write a 700-word blog post for [Business Name] in [city] on the topic: [topic]. The post should: be genuinely helpful to [target customer], naturally include these local terms: [city], [neighborhood], [nearby landmark], be written in a friendly but professional tone, include a brief mention of our services without being salesy, and end with a call to action to visit or contact us. Optimize the post for someone searching [main keyword].'

Examples of topics:
- Restaurant: 'The Best Date Night Spots in [Neighborhood] — And Why We Made the List'
- Gym: 'How to Start Working Out in [City]: A Beginner's Guide'
- Salon: 'The Most Popular Hair Colors in [City] This Season'
- HVAC: 'How to Prepare Your [City] Home for Winter: A Local Contractor's Guide'

## Seasonal Campaign Automation

Build your annual campaign calendar in January. Map out:
- Q1: New Year offers, Valentine's Day, spring preparation
- Q2: Mother's Day, spring cleaning, graduations
- Q3: Summer promos, back-to-school
- Q4: Fall specials, holiday campaigns, year-end

For each campaign, use AI to generate: email subject lines (5 options), email body copy, social media captions (3-4 options), and ad copy.

Schedule everything 2-3 weeks in advance. Seasonal campaigns that go out on the day of the event are too late — you want to be in their inbox before they've made plans.

## Paid Ad Copy with AI

**Meta (Facebook/Instagram) Ads:**

Prompt for ad copy: 'Write 3 Facebook ad copy variations for a [business type] targeting [audience description] in [city]. Each variation should: have a hook in the first line that stops scrolling, address one specific pain point or desire, mention the offer clearly, and include a call to action. Variation 1: emotional/story angle. Variation 2: social proof angle. Variation 3: direct/offer angle. Keep each under 150 words.'

**Google Local Service Ads and Search Ads:**

For the headline (30 characters): Use AI to generate 10 headline options that include your main service keyword and a differentiator. Example: 'Same-Day HVAC Repair | [City]' or 'Award-Winning Italian in [Neighborhood]'

For the description (90 characters): Benefit + proof + action. 'Family-owned since 2004. 500+ 5-star reviews. Book online in 60 seconds.'

sections.title: Staff and Operations Efficiency
sections.subtitle: Run a tighter operation without hiring an operations manager
sections.content:
Operational chaos costs local businesses an estimated 15-25% of potential revenue through mistakes, turnover, and inefficiency. AI cannot fix a bad culture or poor leadership — but it can eliminate the documentation debt, communication gaps, and information bottlenecks that drain your team's time and confidence every week.

## Training Documentation Generation

Most local businesses have tribal knowledge — critical information that lives in one person's head and nowhere else. When that person leaves, their knowledge walks out with them. AI solves this by making documentation fast enough that it actually gets done.

**The SOP Generation Process:**

1. Voice record yourself (or a skilled employee) explaining how to do the task. Use your phone's Voice Memos app. A 3-minute explanation is enough.
2. Transcribe the recording using Whisper (free at OpenAI) or Otter.ai ($10/month).
3. Paste the transcript into ChatGPT with this prompt: 'Turn this rough spoken explanation into a professional Standard Operating Procedure document. Format it with: title, purpose, who this applies to, materials/tools needed, step-by-step numbered instructions (numbered, not bulleted), what to do if something goes wrong, and a checklist at the bottom. Keep language clear and simple for someone on their first week of work. Raw transcript: [paste transcript]'
4. Review for accuracy (the AI will get the structure right; you verify the details).
5. Save to a shared folder (Google Drive works perfectly).

Target: Document your top 10 procedures in the first month. Then add as needed.

**Training Materials Generation:**

For onboarding new hires: 'Create a first-week training checklist for a new [role] at a [business type]. Include: Day 1 orientation items, Day 2-3 shadowing tasks, Day 4-5 supervised work tasks, and end-of-week check-in questions. Also include a list of questions the new employee should be able to answer by the end of week one.'

For product or service knowledge: 'Create a product knowledge quiz for a [staff role] at a [business type]. Generate 15 multiple-choice questions covering: [list your products/services, policies, and procedures]. Include the answer key. Difficulty: someone who has completed 3 days of training should score 85%.'

## Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) Creation

SOP categories every local business needs documented (prioritized):

**Priority 1 — Customer-Facing Procedures:**
- How to greet and check in a new customer
- How to handle a complaint in person
- Opening and closing procedures
- How to process a refund or exchange

**Priority 2 — Operational:**
- How to clean and maintain equipment
- Inventory counting and ordering procedure
- Emergency procedures (power outage, no-show supplier, equipment failure)
- Cash handling and POS procedures

**Priority 3 — Marketing and Communications:**
- How to post on social media (including the brand voice guide)
- How to respond to reviews (link to your response library)
- How to request a review from a happy customer

For each SOP, use the voice-to-document process above. Total time investment: 15-20 minutes per document once you are in the habit.

**Brand Voice Guide (generated with AI):**
Prompt: 'Based on this description of our business [describe your business, customers, and values], create a brand voice guide for our staff. Include: 3 words that describe our tone, 5 examples of phrases we say vs. phrases we never say, guidelines for texting customers vs. emailing customers, and how to handle situations where the customer is wrong.'

## Staff Communication and Scheduling Assistance

AI does not replace your scheduling software — it helps you communicate around it.

**Schedule-Related Templates:**

For posting schedules: 'Write an announcement to post in our staff group chat sharing this week's schedule. Make it clear, include the schedule information, highlight any special events or busy periods, and end on a positive note. Schedule details: [paste schedule]'

For coverage requests: 'Write a message to send to a team when we need to find coverage for a shift on [date and time]. Ask for volunteers, provide the relevant details, and include a deadline for responses. Tone: professional but casual, team-oriented.'

For difficult conversations (documentation): 'Help me draft talking points for a performance conversation with an employee who has been [describe behavior]. Include: how to open the conversation, specific observations (not judgments), what I need to change, consequences if not improved, and how to end constructively. Keep it factual and non-confrontational.'

**Staff Scheduling Tools:**
- Homebase (free for one location): Scheduling, time clock, and team messaging
- 7shifts (free up to 10 employees): Built for restaurants
- When I Work ($2.50/employee/month): Clean interface, good mobile app
- Deputy ($4.50/employee/month): More robust for compliance-heavy businesses

## Inventory and Supply Management

AI cannot manage your inventory for you, but it can help you build the systems that do it accurately.

**The Inventory Audit Prompt:**
'Create an inventory tracking spreadsheet template for a [business type]. Columns should include: item name, SKU/code, unit of measure, current quantity, reorder point, reorder quantity, primary supplier, backup supplier, cost per unit, and last ordered date. Also create a simple formula guide for calculating days-of-supply remaining based on average weekly usage.'

**Supplier Communication Templates:**
For reorder emails: 'Write a professional but brief purchase order email for ordering [item] from [supplier]. I need [quantity] units. Include: order details, our preferred delivery date, our account/business name, and a request to confirm receipt and estimated delivery.'

For supplier issues: 'Write a professional email to a supplier about a delivery that arrived with [issue: wrong items / damaged / late]. Keep it firm but collaborative, request a resolution, and ask for confirmation of their response timeline.'

## Financial Reporting and Cash Flow Analysis

AI cannot replace your accountant. It can help you understand your numbers faster and ask better questions.

**The Weekly Financial Summary Prompt:**
Export your POS or accounting data to a spreadsheet. Then paste summary figures into AI with this prompt: 'Analyze this week's revenue data for my [business type] and provide: comparison to last week (percentage change), top 3 revenue drivers, any concerning trends, and 2-3 questions I should ask my accountant or look into further. Data: [paste figures]'

**Cash Flow Forecasting:**
'Create a simple cash flow projection spreadsheet template for a [business type] with seasonal revenue patterns. Include rows for: revenue by category, fixed expenses, variable expenses, owner's draw, tax reserve, and net cash position. Add a notes column and a 3-month rolling view.'

## Customer Feedback Synthesis

If you collect customer surveys or have a significant review volume, AI can analyze them faster than reading each one.

**Monthly Feedback Analysis Prompt:**
'Read these customer reviews/feedback comments and provide: the top 3 compliments mentioned most frequently, the top 3 complaints or concerns mentioned most frequently, 2-3 specific operational recommendations based on the feedback, and the overall sentiment score (positive/neutral/negative breakdown). Feedback: [paste 20-30 reviews or survey responses]'

Run this monthly. Track the themes over time in a simple spreadsheet. When the same complaint appears 3 months in a row, it is no longer an outlier — it is a system problem that needs fixing.

restaurant.title: The Restaurant AI Playbook
restaurant.subtitle: From menu to reservations to social media — AI tools built for food service
restaurant.intro: Running a restaurant means managing food costs, staff, customers, and online reputation simultaneously — often while standing in a kitchen. This playbook is written for owners and managers who need practical tools, not theory. Every recommendation here has been tested in real restaurant operations.

menu_description_optimization.title: Menu Description Optimization: The Words That Sell
menu_description_optimization.body:
Menu language directly impacts average check size. A study of restaurant menus found that descriptive menu items (using sensory, geographic, or nostalgic language) sell 27% more than neutral descriptions. AI makes rewriting your entire menu possible in an afternoon.

**The Menu Description Prompt That Increases Order Value:**
'Rewrite this menu item description to be more compelling and appetizing. Use: sensory language (texture, temperature, aroma), the origin of a key ingredient if notable, the cooking method in an appealing way, and end with what makes it special. Keep it under 30 words. Do not use words like: fresh, delicious, amazing, or tasty (these are overused and meaningless). Item: [current description]'

**Example transformation:**
Before: 'Grilled Salmon — Fresh salmon with lemon and herbs. Served with vegetables. $24'
After: 'Pacific King Salmon — Seared over hardwood until crisp-skinned, finished with charred lemon butter and garden herbs. Served with roasted broccolini. $24'

**Price Anchoring Through Description:**
Your highest-margin item should have your longest, most sensory description. Your lowest-margin item should have your shortest description. AI can help you audit this across your full menu.

**Seasonal Menu Planning Prompt:**
'Help me build a seasonal autumn menu for a [cuisine type] restaurant. We focus on [food values, e.g., locally sourced, comfort food, bold flavors]. Suggest: 3 appetizers, 4 entrees, and 2 desserts that use autumn-available ingredients (squash, apples, root vegetables, mushrooms). For each, provide a 25-word description and a suggested price point for a [positioning, e.g., casual, upscale casual, fine dining] restaurant in a mid-sized city.'

**Delivery Platform Optimization (DoorDash, Uber Eats, Grubhub):**
Your delivery menu descriptions need to overcome a critical disadvantage: customers can't smell or see the food in front of them. Descriptions carry more weight here than in your physical space.

Prompt: 'Rewrite this menu item for a delivery platform listing where customers are choosing from 20+ restaurants and cannot see or smell the food. Make the description: scannable in 3 seconds, appetite-triggering at first glance, and reassuring about quality (since delivery customers worry about food arriving well). Item: [item name and current description]'

Also review your delivery menu photography. Poor photos cost you more orders than any other factor. If you cannot afford a professional shoot, use this workflow: natural light near a window, shoot from directly above (flat lay) or at a 45-degree angle, and use the free Lightroom Mobile preset 'Food - Warm' to enhance colors.

review_response_templates.title: Restaurant-Specific Review Response Templates
review_response_templates.body:
Restaurants get the most specific and emotionally charged reviews of any local business. 'The steak was cold,' 'We waited 45 minutes,' 'Wrong order and nobody apologized' — these need precise responses that acknowledge without overpromising.

**Cold Food Complaints:**
'[Name], thank you for the honest feedback. Cold food is never acceptable, and we want every dish to arrive at the table exactly as our kitchen intended it. This isn't the standard we hold ourselves to, and I apologize it fell short for you. We'd like the chance to make it right — please reach out to me directly at [email/phone] and I'll ensure your next visit is exceptional.'

**Wait Time Complaints:**
'[Name], we appreciate you sharing this with us. There's no good excuse for a wait time that doesn't match your expectations, and we're always working to improve our pacing, especially during peak hours. Your patience means a lot, and we're sorry this visit wasn't what it should have been. We'd love another opportunity — feel free to mention this when you call for your next reservation, and we'll take good care of you.'

**Wrong Order Complaints:**
'[Name], receiving the wrong order is genuinely frustrating and we take full responsibility when that happens. Our kitchen and service standards should prevent this, and clearly we fell short with you. Please reach out to [name] at [phone/email] — I want to hear exactly what happened and ensure this is addressed both for you and for our team's training.'

**AI Prompt for Custom Responses:**
'Write a response to this Google review for our restaurant. Address the specific issue mentioned (do not be defensive or make excuses), acknowledge their experience genuinely, mention one thing we are doing to improve this, invite them back with a personal note (not a discount offer), and keep it under 100 words. Our restaurant's tone is: [warm/professional/casual]. Review: [paste review]'

social_media_content.title: Social Media Content for Restaurants
social_media_content.body:
Restaurant social media works when it makes people hungry or curious. The formats that consistently perform:

**1. Plating Process Videos (Reels/TikTok)**
A 15-second clip of a dish being plated with a trending audio track. Shot on iPhone. No editing required. This format gets 5-10x more reach than a static food photo. Post 2-3 per week.

Caption prompt: 'Write a caption for a plating video of [dish name] at our restaurant. Make it make people hungry in the first line. Include what makes this dish special. End with a question or a call to book. Under 100 words.'

**2. Behind-the-Kitchen Content**
Introduce your chef. Show a technique. Reveal what goes into a signature dish. This builds trust and differentiation that no ad can replicate.

Caption prompt: 'Write a caption for a behind-the-scenes video showing [kitchen moment/technique]. Our restaurant is [describe vibe/cuisine]. Make it feel like an insider look — exciting, genuine, and a little proud. Under 80 words.'

**3. Special and Limited-Time Offer Posts**
These drive direct revenue. Specificity is key: 'Available this weekend only' beats 'Check out our new special.'

Caption prompt: 'Write 3 caption options for a social media post announcing [name of special], available [timeframe]. Each caption should: create urgency, describe the dish using appetite-triggering language, and end with how to order or reserve. Variations: one casual, one story-driven, one direct/urgent.'

**4. User-Generated Content Reposts**
When customers post photos of your food and tag you, repost them (with credit). This is free content and social proof. Encourage tagging by placing a small card on tables: 'Tag us for a chance to be featured.'

**Posting Schedule for Restaurants:**
- Monday: Staff/kitchen highlight (humanizes the brand)
- Wednesday: Food content (plating video or signature dish)
- Friday: Weekend special announcement
- Saturday: User-generated content or event reminder
- Sunday: 'Brunch is live' or 'Sunday special' — highly engaged day for restaurant content

reservation_system.title: Reservation System Integration
reservation_system.body:
If you are not on OpenTable or Resy, you are invisible to a significant segment of customers who discover restaurants through these platforms rather than Google.

**OpenTable:** $249/month but includes significant discovery benefit (OpenTable users book directly through the app). Best for higher-volume restaurants.

**Resy:** Competitive pricing, strong in food-culture cities. Good for waitlist management.

**Google Reservations (via Reserve with Google):** Free. Integrates directly with Google Search and Maps results. Works through booking partners like OpenTable, Resy, or Yelp. If you are already on one of these, enable the Google integration.

**Cheaper option:** TableAgent (free) or Yelp Waitlist (included with Yelp Business) for basic reservation management.

**Reservation Confirmation + Reminder Automation:**
All major platforms send confirmations automatically. Ensure your confirmation message includes:
- Parking instructions
- Cancellation policy with clear window
- Your phone number for special requests
- A note about what to expect (dress code if relevant, any prep for large party)

**AI Prompt for Reservation Confirmation:**
'Write a reservation confirmation email/text for our restaurant. Include: a warm greeting, confirmation of date/time/party size, our address and brief parking note, cancellation policy (24 hours notice), a line inviting them to call for special requests, and a genuine statement about looking forward to having them. Keep it under 150 words. Warm, not corporate.'

staff_scheduling.title: Staff Scheduling and Tip Reporting
staff_scheduling.body:
**Scheduling Tools for Restaurants:**
7shifts (free up to 10 employees, then $17.99/month) is purpose-built for restaurants with POS integrations and tip pooling features. It also has a team communication channel.

Homebase (free for 1 location) is broader but very functional for schedule creation, time tracking, and payroll prep.

**AI-Assisted Scheduling Prompt:**
'Help me build a restaurant schedule for [week of date]. Staff: [list staff names, roles, and any constraints they've given you]. Business needs: [busiest days, any events, any special requirements]. Rules: minimum [X] servers per shift, kitchen always has [X] people, at least one senior staff member per shift. Generate a draft schedule in a table format.'

The AI draft will not be perfect — you will need to adjust for personalities, labor law, and things the AI doesn't know. But it reduces your scheduling time from 90 minutes to 20 minutes.

**Tip Reporting Documentation:**
'Create a tip reporting log template for our restaurant staff. Include columns for: date, shift (AM/PM), server name, total sales, credit card tips, cash tips received, tip pool contribution (percentage), tip pool received, and total take-home tips. Add a weekly summary row that calculates totals and averages per server.'

three_immediate_wins.title: 3 Immediate Wins for Restaurant Owners — Do These Today

wins.title: Win 1: Automate Your Post-Dining Review Request (30 minutes to set up)
wins.description: Get your Google review link (GBP > Get more reviews > Copy). If you use a texting tool (even Google Voice), create a template text: 'Thank you for dining with us at [Restaurant]! If you have a minute, a Google review means the world to us: [link]. Hope to see you again soon.' Send this manually to every diner whose number you have for the next 7 days. Most restaurants generate 8-15 new reviews in the first week of doing this. After 7 days of manual sending, automate it through your POS or reservation system's post-visit messaging feature.

wins.title: Win 2: Rewrite Your 3 Best-Selling Menu Items With AI (45 minutes)
wins.description: Open ChatGPT. Use the menu description prompt above to rewrite your top 3 dishes. Update your physical menu, your delivery platform listing, and your Google Business Profile's Products section. Take photos of those dishes today if yours aren't strong. Track whether those items' order frequency changes over the next 30 days. Most restaurants see a 10-20% increase in orders for items with improved descriptions and photos.

wins.title: Win 3: Build Your 2-Week Social Media Content Batch (2 hours)
wins.description: Using the batch content system above, generate 10-14 days of captions. Film 3-4 short plating or kitchen videos on your iPhone. Schedule them in Meta Business Suite (free). You are now 2 weeks ahead on content with zero agency involvement. Repeat this every other Monday for the next month and it becomes a habit.

salon_spa.title: The Salon & Spa AI Playbook
salon_spa.subtitle: Keep your chair full and your clients coming back — with half the admin work
salon_spa.intro: The salon and spa industry runs on relationships, repeat visits, and word-of-mouth. AI does not change that equation — it removes the friction that keeps clients from rebooking and frees you to focus on the actual service. Whether you are a solo stylist or managing a team, these tools are specifically designed for your world.

appointment_booking.title: Appointment Booking Automation
appointment_booking.body:
Salons lose more revenue to 'I meant to call but forgot' than any other factor. Online booking eliminates that friction entirely.

**Recommended Booking Tools:**
- Vagaro ($25-85/month): Purpose-built for beauty professionals. Includes booking, POS, client cards, email marketing, and app listing (Vagaro Marketplace exposes you to new clients).
- Fresha (free): Zero subscription fee. Takes a small commission on new clients found through the platform. Good for solo stylists just starting out.
- Square Appointments (free for individuals, $29+/month for teams): Familiar interface, integrates with Square POS.
- Mindbody ($129/month): Best for spas with multiple services and staff.

**Booking Confirmation Message (customize in your tool):**
'Hi [Client Name]! Your appointment with [Stylist] is confirmed for [Day], [Date] at [Time] for [Service]. We're at [Address]. Free parking [details]. If you need to reschedule, please do so at least 24 hours in advance: [rescheduling link]. Can't wait to see you! — [Salon Name]'

**48-Hour Reminder:**
'Reminder: You're in with [Stylist] tomorrow at [Time] for [Service]. If you need to make changes, do so by [time] today: [link]. See you then!'

**Same-Day Reminder:**
'See you today at [Time], [Client Name]! We're located at [Address]. If running late, give us a quick call at [phone] so we can adjust. Looking forward to it!'

client_consultation.title: Client Consultation AI: Pre-Appointment Questionnaires
client_consultation.body:
Pre-appointment intake forms save chair time and improve service quality dramatically. Clients arrive knowing what they want; you arrive knowing what to prepare.

**Build a digital intake form using:** Typeform (free tier), Google Forms (free), or the intake form feature in Vagaro or Mindbody.

**Color Service Pre-Consultation Form Questions (generate variations with AI):**
- What is your current hair color and when was it last done professionally?
- What is your goal for today? (Attach photo references)
- How much maintenance are you willing to do at home?
- Any previous reactions to color products?
- Budget range for today's service and ongoing maintenance?

**Prompt to Generate a Full Intake Form:**
'Create a pre-appointment client consultation form for a [hair salon / esthetician / nail salon / massage spa]. Include questions about: current state, desired outcome, lifestyle considerations (how much time they spend on maintenance), previous services/reactions, and any health considerations relevant to this service type. Format as a clean list of questions I can put into a Google Form.'

**Using AI to Analyze Consultation Notes:**
After a consultation, voice-record your notes on what the client said. Then use AI to turn them into a structured client card entry: 'Turn these consultation notes into a structured client profile I can save in my booking system. Include: services discussed, agreed plan, products recommended, notes for next visit, and follow-up timing. Notes: [paste transcript]'

rebooking_retention.title: Rebooking and Retention Campaigns
rebooking_retention.body:
The most profitable clients are existing clients. Studies across the beauty industry show it costs 5x more to acquire a new client than to retain an existing one.

**The Rebooking Script (say this in the chair):**
At the end of every service, before the client leaves the chair: 'So based on what we did today, you'll want to come back in about [6-8 weeks / 4 weeks / 12 weeks] to keep this looking great. Want to go ahead and lock that in at the front before you leave?'

The in-person rebooking conversion rate is 60-70%. The 'we'll call you' follow-up rate is under 20%.

**Automated Re-Engagement for Lapsed Clients:**

Set up a trigger in your booking system: if a client has not booked in [X weeks — use the typical service interval for their last service], send this message:

'Hi [Name], it's been a while and we miss you! Your [highlights/cut/etc.] from [approximate date] is probably ready for some attention. We have some availability coming up — book your spot here: [link]. Hope to see you soon!'

**Seasonal Retention Campaign:**
'Write a text/email to send to salon clients we haven't seen in [60/90] days. Service type: [hair, nails, skincare]. Time of year: [season]. Include: a reference to a seasonal service, a gentle call to action with a booking link, and a personal, warm tone — not a blast-email tone. No discount offers (we don't discount our services). Under 100 words.'

product_recommendation.title: Product Recommendation Automation
product_recommendation.body:
Retail product sales are often 20-30% of a salon's revenue potential but consistently underperformed. AI helps by building the recommendation workflow into your process.

**Post-Appointment Product Follow-Up Text:**
Send 24 hours after appointment: 'Hi [Name]! Hope you're loving your [service]. A reminder of what [Stylist] recommended for home care: [Product 1] for [benefit], [Product 2] for [benefit]. You can grab these at the salon or order through our online store: [link]. Questions? Just reply here.'

**Generate Product Education Content:**
Prompt: 'Write 5 Instagram/Facebook posts educating clients about [product name or category] for [hair/skin type]. Each post should: explain a real benefit in plain language (no marketing jargon), include a tip for using it at home, and end with a soft call to purchase or ask their stylist. Not salesy — educational.'

**Email Template for New Product Arrival:**
Subject: 'Something new just arrived at [Salon Name]'
Body: 'Hey [First Name], we just got [product name] in the salon and I wanted you to know about it first because it's perfect for [specific hair/skin concern]. Here's what it does: [3 bullet points]. If you want to try it before committing to a full size, ask us about [sample/small size] at your next appointment. Book here: [link]. — [Stylist/Owner name]'

before_after_content.title: Before/After Content Strategy
before_after_content.body:
Before/after transformations are the highest-converting content type in the beauty industry. They are proof of skill, not just claims.

**Process:**
1. Always take a before photo at the beginning of the appointment (with client permission).
2. Take an after photo immediately before the client leaves — do not skip this even when busy.
3. Batch upload at the end of each week, not daily.

**Caption Prompt for Before/After Posts:**
'Write a caption for a before/after transformation post. Service performed: [describe]. Client's starting point: [describe]. The goal: [describe]. Include: what made this transformation technically interesting or challenging (show expertise), tag the client if they agreed, and end with an availability call to action. No generic phrases like 'love my job' or 'transformation Tuesday.' Under 120 words.'

**Client Permission Script:**
'Would you mind if I posted a before/after of your transformation? I'd love to show off the work — and if you'd prefer, I can skip tagging your account and just post the photos.' Most clients say yes because they are proud of their results.

review_generation.title: Review Generation Specific to Beauty Services
review_generation.body:
The golden moment for a salon review request is right after the reveal — the moment the client sees themselves in the mirror and reacts positively.

**In-Salon QR Code:**
Place a small framed card at the styling station with your Google review QR code and this text: 'Love your new look? Leave us a quick review — it means the world to our team.'

**Automated Text (24 hours post-appointment):**
'Hi [Name], it was so great having you in yesterday! If you're loving your [service], we'd be incredibly grateful for a quick Google review: [link]. It only takes a minute and helps our small business more than you know. Thank you!'

**What to Ask Clients to Include (you can mention this conversationally):**
'If you do leave a review, feel free to mention [stylist name] — Google reviews that mention the specific service and stylist really help people find us when they're searching.'

staff_training.title: Staff Education and Training Materials
staff_training.body:
**New Stylist Onboarding Document Generator:**
'Create a 30-day onboarding plan for a new hairstylist joining our team. Include: Week 1 (observation and orientation), Week 2 (assisted services), Week 3 (supervised independent work), Week 4 (full book under mentorship). For each week, include daily goals, skills to demonstrate by end of week, and how the owner/manager will evaluate progress.'

**Technical Training Document:**
'Create a step-by-step training guide for [technique, e.g., balayage application, gel manicure removal, facial extraction protocol]. Format as: what you need, step-by-step numbered instructions, common mistakes to avoid, and a checklist for confirming the service is done correctly.'

**Service Menu Education:**
'Create a staff training quiz for a [salon/spa] covering our service menu. Generate 20 questions that a new team member should be able to answer after 2 weeks of training. Topics: service timing, pricing, contraindications, what to recommend for different client concerns, and our booking and cancellation policies.'

three_immediate_wins.title: 3 Immediate Wins for Salon Owners — Do These Today

wins.title: Win 1: Turn On Online Booking and Add a 48-Hour Reminder (1 hour)
wins.description: If you are not on an online booking platform yet, sign up for Fresha (free) or Square Appointments today. Configure a 48-hour reminder text for all existing and future bookings. Track your no-show rate for the next 30 days. Most salons see a 30-50% reduction in no-shows immediately. If you already have online booking but the reminders are not turned on — turn them on right now. This is a 3-minute task that directly recovers lost revenue.

wins.title: Win 2: Build a 60-Day Re-Engagement Campaign (45 minutes)
wins.description: Export your client list. Filter for clients whose last appointment was more than 60 days ago (or more than the typical interval for their service type). Use the lapsed client re-engagement template above. Send it as a text or email to this segment. Include a direct booking link. Set a reminder to check how many booked within 2 weeks. Most salons recover 10-20% of lapsed clients with one well-written re-engagement message.

wins.title: Win 3: Create a Before/After Content Pipeline (30 minutes setup, ongoing)
wins.description: Print permission cards to hand to clients at the start of color or dramatic transformation appointments. Get verbal consent, take the before photo, take the after photo. End of week: upload to Instagram/Facebook using the caption prompt above. Do this every week for a month. Your Instagram will transform, your booking inquiries from social will increase, and you will have documented proof of your skill level that no ad can replicate.

gym_fitness.title: The Gym & Fitness AI Playbook
gym_fitness.subtitle: Reduce churn, fill your classes, and grow your member base without burning out
gym_fitness.intro: Gyms and fitness studios face two core business challenges: getting new members in the door, and keeping existing members from canceling. AI primarily helps with the second problem — the retention crisis that costs most fitness businesses 30-50% of their member base every year. This playbook gives you the tools to fight attrition with automated, personal-feeling touchpoints that scale.

member_onboarding.title: Member Onboarding Automation
member_onboarding.body:
The first 30 days of a membership determine whether someone stays or cancels. Most gyms drop the ball in this window because onboarding is handled manually and inconsistently.

**The 30-Day New Member Sequence:**

Day 1 — Welcome Text:
'Welcome to [Gym Name], [First Name]! We're so glad you're here. Your member portal: [link]. To book your complimentary [orientation/PT session/class], use this link: [link]. Any questions, reply here or call [phone]. Let's make this your year.'

Day 3 — App or Booking Reminder:
'Hey [Name], have you had a chance to book your [free class/orientation]? Here's a direct link: [link]. The [day/time] sessions fill up fast. See you on the floor!'

Day 7 — Check-In Text:
'[Name], it's been one week! How's it going? If you have any questions about equipment, classes, or your membership, I'm always reachable here. Also — what fitness goal are you working toward? Happy to point you in the right direction.'

Day 14 — Value-Add:
'[Name], here's something our most successful members do in week two: [specific tip — e.g., book 3 classes in advance to cement the habit, download our app to track workouts, join our private Facebook group for challenges and nutrition tips]. Give it a shot this week!'

Day 30 — Milestone Acknowledgment:
'One month in, [Name]! That puts you in the top 30% of members who make it this far. You've built something real. What's your goal for month two? Reply and let us know — we'll keep you accountable.'

Build this in your CRM or email platform (Mailchimp, Klaviyo, or your gym software's automation feature). Mindbody, Glofox, and Pike13 all support drip sequences.

training_program_generation.title: Personal Training Program Generation
training_program_generation.body:
For trainers, writing individual programs is one of the most time-consuming tasks. AI can generate a base program in 2 minutes that a trainer then customizes — cutting program-writing time from 45 minutes to 10.

**Personal Training Program Prompt:**
'Create a 4-week beginner strength training program for a [gender, age] client with the following profile: current fitness level [beginner/some experience], main goal [weight loss/strength/athletic performance], available days [3x/week], equipment available [full gym/dumbbells only/etc.], any injuries or limitations [list]. Format: week-by-week, day-by-day, with exercises listed as: exercise name, sets x reps, rest time, and a brief coaching note. Include a warm-up and cool-down routine.'

**For Group Class Programming:**
'Create a 4-week HIIT class program for a boutique fitness studio. Classes are 45 minutes, 3x per week. Participants range from intermediate to advanced. Each week should have a different theme or focus area. Format each class as: warm-up (5 min), main workout (30 min with circuits or intervals), cool-down (10 min). Include modifications for lower-impact participants.'

**Nutrition Content Generation:**
For gyms offering nutrition guidance (not personalized medical advice): 'Create a 7-day meal prep guide for someone focused on [fat loss/muscle building/general wellness]. Include: daily macro targets, 3 meal options per day, a weekly shopping list, and 3 simple meal prep tips. Keep it practical for someone cooking 1-2 times per week.'

retention_campaigns.title: Retention Campaigns: Targeting At-Risk Members
retention_campaigns.body:
An at-risk member is someone who has stopped using the gym but is still paying. The warning signs:
- Has not checked in for 14+ days (at-risk)
- Has not checked in for 30+ days (likely to cancel)
- Has not checked in for 45+ days (cancel imminent without intervention)

**The At-Risk Member Message Sequence:**

14-Day No-Show Text:
'Hey [Name], we noticed you haven't been in for a couple weeks — life gets busy, totally get it. Is there anything getting in the way? New class schedule, a goal you want to refocus on, or just need someone to chat with about getting back? Reply here and we'll help. No pressure.'

30-Day No-Show Text:
'[Name], it's been a while and we genuinely want to help you get back on track. Sometimes the hardest part is just walking back through the door. We want to make that easier — would a [free PT session / free class / check-in call with a trainer] help? Just say yes and we'll set it up.'

45-Day Emergency Intervention:
'[Name], your membership is coming up for renewal soon. Before that happens, we want to make sure it's still the right fit for you. Can we get on a quick 10-minute call to talk about your goals and how we can better support you? Book here: [Calendly link]. No obligation. We genuinely want to help.'

**Prompt for Personalized Retention Messages:**
'Write a re-engagement text for a gym member who: last visited [X days ago], originally joined to [stated goal at signup], has attended [class type] most frequently. Tone: personal and concerned, not corporate or sales-y. Do not offer a discount — instead offer value (a free PT session, a class recommendation, a check-in call). Under 80 words.'

class_optimization.title: Class Schedule Optimization
class_optimization.body:
Data you likely already have in your gym software tells you: which classes fill up, which consistently have open spots, and what times your members prefer. Use AI to help analyze and act on it.

**Class Performance Analysis Prompt:**
'Analyze this class attendance data and identify: the top 3 classes by average fill rate, the bottom 3 classes by fill rate, the best performing time slots, and 3 recommendations for schedule changes that would likely improve overall attendance and satisfaction. Data: [paste your class attendance report or summary]'

**Waitlist Management:**
For popular classes, enable waitlists in your software. Send an automated message when a spot opens:
'Good news, [Name] — a spot just opened in [Class Name] on [date] at [time]. Book it before it fills up: [link]. First come, first served!'

**New Class Launch Promotion:**
'Write an announcement to send to existing members about a new class we are adding to the schedule: [class name, description, time, days]. Include: what makes this class different, who it's best for, and a call to action to sign up for the first session. Tone: excited but not hype-y. Under 150 words.'

community_engagement.title: Challenge and Community Engagement Automation
community_engagement.body:
Member communities and challenges significantly reduce churn. Members with friends at the gym cancel at 40% the rate of isolated members.

**The 30-Day Challenge Framework:**
Use AI to build a full challenge program: 'Create a 30-day fitness challenge for a gym member community. The challenge should: have a clear theme [e.g., consistency, strength, weight loss jump-start], have daily or weekly milestones, be appropriate for various fitness levels, include a social element (tag a workout partner, post a check-in), and build to a visible result by day 30. Format as a calendar with daily prompts and weekly goals. Include: week 1 social media posts announcing the challenge, a sign-up form structure, and a celebration/acknowledgment plan for completers.'

**Private Facebook Group Content Calendar:**
For gyms with a private member Facebook group or community (highly recommended if you don't have one): 'Create a 4-week content calendar for a gym's private Facebook group. Content types: motivational Monday, transformation Tuesday (member spotlight), workout Wednesday tip, Thursday Q&A prompt, Friday wins sharing, Saturday challenge progress check. Generate one specific post idea per day for all 28 days. Each post should encourage comment engagement, not just passive reading.'

**Membership Renewal Campaign:**
'Write a 3-message sequence to send to gym members whose membership renews in [30 days / 14 days / 3 days]. Each message should: acknowledge their journey so far (reference [X months] they've been a member), highlight a benefit of continuing (community, progress, access), and include a clear renewal link. Tone: celebratory and forward-looking, not administrative.'

three_immediate_wins.title: 3 Immediate Wins for Gym Owners — Do These Today

wins.title: Win 1: Pull Your At-Risk Member List and Send One Text Today (1 hour)
wins.description: Log into your gym software. Pull a list of members who have not checked in for 14-30 days. Use the 14-day no-show text template above. Send it personally (not as a blast — personalize the first name at minimum). Track how many reply or book. A single well-timed re-engagement text can recover 15-25% of at-risk members. If you have 20 at-risk members at $50/month, recovering 4 of them is $200/month or $2,400/year from one hour of work.

wins.title: Win 2: Build Your 30-Day New Member Sequence (2 hours)
wins.description: Write all 5 messages using the templates above. Load them into your email or CRM platform as a time-delay sequence triggered when someone purchases a new membership. The total setup time is 2 hours. Every new member from this point forward gets a consistent, personal-feeling onboarding experience automatically. Members who complete a full onboarding sequence have a 40-60% higher 90-day retention rate.

wins.title: Win 3: Launch a 30-Day Member Challenge (2 hours of setup, massive engagement return)
wins.description: Use the challenge framework prompt to create a full 30-day challenge. Announce it to your member base via email and text. Create a private Facebook group or use an existing one for accountability posts. The challenge serves three purposes simultaneously: it re-engages at-risk members, deepens community bonds, and gives you 30 days of social media content. Run one challenge per quarter.

real_estate_agency.title: The Real Estate Agency AI Playbook
real_estate_agency.subtitle: Generate more leads, close faster, and spend less time on paperwork
real_estate_agency.intro: Real estate is a relationship business that has become a documentation and marketing business by necessity. Agents spend 40-60% of their working hours on tasks that do not directly generate commission: writing listing descriptions, sending follow-ups, creating market reports, and maintaining client communication. AI reclaims most of that time — and the quality of the output often exceeds what an agent would produce manually.

listing_descriptions.title: Property Listing Description Generation
listing_descriptions.body:
A well-written listing description does two things: it ranks better in search (MLS search, Zillow, Realtor.com) and it emotionally connects with buyers who read it. Most descriptions fail at both.

**The Master Listing Description Prompt:**
'Write a compelling MLS listing description for the following property. Use: an emotionally engaging opening that places the buyer inside the lifestyle of this home, specific architectural or design details (not generic), neighborhood context (walking distance to X, proximity to Y), and a closing line that creates urgency or emotional resolution. Do not use: the words 'stunning,' 'gorgeous,' 'boasts,' 'nestled,' 'charming,' or 'meticulously maintained' — these are clichés that buyers ignore. Keep it under 300 words. Property details: [list every relevant detail: bedrooms, baths, sqft, lot, year built, updates, neighborhood, school district, special features, recent renovations]'

**By Property Type:**

For a starter home/condo:
'Focus the description on lifestyle, convenience, and move-in readiness. The buyer is likely a first-time buyer or young professional. Emphasize: commute convenience, nearby amenities, what they can do immediately vs. what they need to update, and the community feel.'

For a luxury property:
'Lead with the most distinctive architectural or lifestyle feature. Reference specific finishes by brand or origin where notable. Describe the morning and evening light if the lot orientation is favorable. Reference privacy and scale. Avoid specifics on price/value — this should feel aspirational, not transactional.'

For an investment property:
'Lead with numbers: current rental income or income potential, cap rate if applicable, occupancy history. Then describe the physical asset, tenant situation, and any capital improvements. Buyers of investment properties think in spreadsheets — help them do the math quickly.'

For land/lots:
'Describe the development opportunity first. What can be built? What utilities are connected? What are the zoning allowances? Then describe the physical attributes: topography, views, access, proximity. End with the opportunity frame: what does ownership of this land make possible?'

lead_qualification.title: Lead Qualification and Response Automation
lead_qualification.body:
Speed to lead is the most important variable in real estate conversion. Research from Harvard Business Review found that leads responded to within 5 minutes are 100x more likely to convert than those responded to within 30 minutes.

**Immediate Auto-Response for All Inquiries:**
For every portal (Zillow, Realtor.com, your website contact form): set up an immediate auto-response.

'Hi [Name], this is [Agent] from [Agency]. Thanks for your inquiry about [property address or 'homes in X area']. I'll reach out personally within the next [1-2 hours]. In the meantime, here are a few things that might be helpful: [link to listings], [link to buyer/seller guide], [link to book a call]. Looking forward to connecting.'

**Lead Qualification Message Sequence:**

For buyer leads:
First contact: 'Hi [Name], this is [Agent]! Thanks for reaching out. To help me find you the right options, can I ask: Are you looking to buy in the next 30-60 days, or are you just starting to explore? And have you connected with a lender yet, or is that on your list too? No pressure either way — just want to make sure I send you the most relevant properties.'

For seller leads:
First contact: 'Hi [Name], [Agent] here from [Agency]. Thanks for reaching out about selling. Quick question: are you looking to move in the next 3-6 months, or are you just starting to think through timing? I'd love to put together a no-obligation market snapshot for your home — takes me about 20 minutes. Want me to send that over?'

**AI Prompt for Custom Lead Follow-Up:**
'Write a follow-up message for a real estate lead who: inquired about [property or area], hasn't responded to our initial contact, and we want to reconnect without being pushy. Include: a reference to their original inquiry, a new piece of value (market insight, a similar property, a relevant stat), and a soft call to action. Under 100 words. Do not use the phrase 'just following up.''

market_reports.title: Market Report Generation for Clients
market_reports.body:
Monthly or quarterly market reports position you as the local market expert and keep your name in front of clients who are not actively transacting. Most agents either don't send them, or send generic national data.

**Local Market Report Prompt:**
'Write a 400-word local real estate market update for [neighborhood or city] for [month/quarter]. Use this data I'll provide: [paste your MLS data summary: active listings, average days on market, median price, list-to-sale price ratio, month-over-month change, year-over-year change]. Make it: readable by a non-expert, include 2-3 insights (not just raw data), end with what this means for buyers and sellers right now, and feel like it was written by a local market expert, not a data report. Include a section called 'What This Means If You're Thinking of Selling' and 'What This Means If You're Looking to Buy.''

**Client Newsletter Distribution:**
Send this via email to your full database monthly. Subject line options:
- '[City] Real Estate: What's Happening in [Month]'
- 'Market Update: The Number Every [City] Homeowner Needs to See'
- 'Is [City] a Buyer's or Seller's Market Right Now? (The data is in)'

open_house_followup.title: Open House Follow-Up Sequences
open_house_followup.body:
Most open house attendees receive one generic follow-up email, if anything. A personalized multi-touch sequence triples conversion from open house visitor to actual client.
