**The 5-Touch Open House Follow-Up Sequence:**

Touch 1 — Same day, text (2 hours after open house closes):
'Hi [Name], thanks for stopping by [address] today! Great to meet you. If any questions come up about the property or the neighborhood, feel free to text me here anytime. — [Agent]'

Touch 2 — Day 1, email:
Subject: '[Address] — A few things I forgot to mention'
Body: 'Hi [Name], I'm glad you had a chance to see [address]. A few things I wanted to follow up on: [1-2 interesting features or facts about the property or neighborhood you mentioned in conversation, or that commonly interest buyers]. Also — here are 3 similar properties that might be worth a look if this one interests you: [3 comparable listings]. Happy to set up private showings this week if any catch your eye.'

Touch 3 — Day 3, text:
'Hey [Name], just wanted to check in — any questions about [address] or the [neighborhood] area that I can answer? Also letting you know there's been [note any relevant activity: offers, price drop, strong interest] since the open house.'

Touch 4 — Day 7, value email:
'Hi [Name], sharing the market update for [neighborhood] this week — [attach or link your market snapshot]. Also put together a shortlist of [3-4 homes] in your stated range and preferences. Would love to show you a couple this weekend if you're up for it. What works for your schedule?'

Touch 5 — Day 14, long-term nurture:
'[Name], just checking in to see where you are in your search. No pressure at all — real estate timelines are fluid. If you're still exploring, I'm happy to keep sending relevant listings as they hit the market. Just reply 'yes' and I'll set up an auto-match. If the timing has changed, no worries — I'll be here when it makes sense.'

cma_assistance.title: CMA (Comparative Market Analysis) Assistance
cma_assistance.body:
**Using AI to Narrate a CMA:**
AI cannot run your CMA data — that comes from your MLS. What AI can do is turn raw CMA data into a compelling narrative that builds seller confidence and justifies your recommended list price.

Prompt: 'Turn this Comparative Market Analysis data into a clear, persuasive narrative for a home seller. The narrative should: explain what a CMA is in simple terms, walk through how the comparable properties were selected, explain what the data suggests about pricing strategy, give a concrete recommended list price with a brief rationale, and address the most common objection (seller thinks the home is worth more than the data supports). Data: [paste your CMA figures]'

**Seller Pricing Conversation Script:**
'Generate talking points for a listing consultation where the data supports a price of $[X] but the seller expects $[X + 10-15%]. Include: how to present the market data objectively, how to explain the cost of overpricing (longer days on market, eventual price reduction, stigma), how to present a pricing strategy (list at market, review in 14 days), and how to handle the objection 'but my neighbor sold for X.' Tone: confident and empathetic, not confrontational.'

client_relationship.title: Client Relationship Maintenance Automation
client_relationship.body:
The referral business every agent wants comes from staying top-of-mind with past clients year-round, not just at transaction time.

**Annual Client Touchpoint Calendar (AI-generated):**
Prompt: 'Create a 12-month touchpoint calendar for a real estate agent's past client database. For each month, suggest: one automated email or text topic, one phone call occasion or prompt, and one value-add (market update, local event, home tip). Make it feel local, seasonal, and genuinely helpful — not promotional. Include anniversary of home purchase as a special touchpoint.'

**Home Anniversary Message:**
'Hi [Name]! Hard to believe it's been [X year(s)] since you closed on [address]. Hope you're still loving it. If you ever want an updated market value on the home — just for your own awareness — I'm happy to put together a quick estimate. No obligation, just a useful number to have. How's everything going?'

**Holiday / Seasonal Messages:**
Prompt: 'Write a [season/holiday] message from a real estate agent to their client database. It should feel personal and local, briefly mention a real estate angle (without being sales-y), and end with a warm personal note. Not a mass-email tone — something someone might actually send a friend. Under 100 words.'

three_immediate_wins.title: 3 Immediate Wins for Real Estate Agents — Do These Today

wins.title: Win 1: Rewrite Your Top Active Listing Description (30 minutes)
wins.description: Take your current listing with the most traffic but fewest showings. Use the listing description prompt above with property-specific details. Write two versions: one for the MLS (formal) and one for Zillow/social media (more lifestyle-focused). Update the listing. Track showing requests over the next 7 days versus the previous 7 days. Agents who do this consistently report 20-40% more showing requests from listings with stronger descriptions.

wins.title: Win 2: Build Your Open House Follow-Up Sequence Template (1 hour)
wins.description: Write all 5 follow-up messages using the templates above. Save them in a Google Doc with blanks for personalization. After every future open house, you will have a ready-to-use sequence that takes 10 minutes to personalize and send. Set calendar reminders for Day 1, 3, 7, and 14 follow-ups. Agents with a structured follow-up sequence convert 2-3x more open house visitors than agents who send one generic email.

wins.title: Win 3: Set Up Your Monthly Market Report Email (2 hours, runs automatically after)
wins.description: Get your MLS data summary for your primary market area. Use the market report prompt to write your first report. Email it to your full database with a personal introduction. Set a recurring monthly calendar event to update the data and send a new one. After 3-4 months, you will be known as the agent who actually sends useful local market information — and that reputation generates referral calls.

trades.title: The Trades Business AI Playbook
trades.subtitle: For plumbers, electricians, HVAC contractors, landscapers, and other field service businesses
trades.intro: Trades businesses are the most underserved segment in local business AI adoption. The owners are skilled at their craft, not at marketing or admin — but they are often running $500K-$3M operations while managing their business communications on a flip phone and a legal pad. AI does not change what you do in the field. It changes how you win jobs, follow up with customers, and build the kind of online reputation that keeps the phone ringing.

quote_generation.title: Quote and Estimate Generation
quote_generation.body:
Inconsistent or slow quoting loses jobs. Customers often choose a contractor who sent a clear, professional estimate quickly over a better contractor who took three days to follow up.

**AI-Assisted Quote Template Builder:**
Prompt: 'Create a professional quote/estimate template for a [trade: plumbing / electrical / HVAC / landscaping / etc.] business. Include sections for: customer information, job site address, job description (space for detailed scope of work), itemized materials with quantity and unit price, labor hours and rate, any subcontractor costs, total, payment terms, warranty terms, signature and acceptance line, and a brief 'What happens next' section. Make it look professional even when printed from a basic printer.'

**Quote Description Generator:**
When writing the scope of work, use AI to translate your field notes into clear customer language:
'Turn these field technician notes into a clear, professional scope of work description that a homeowner can understand (no jargon). Make it: specific enough that the customer knows exactly what they're getting, professional in tone, and sequenced logically from start to finish. Notes: [paste your rough notes]'

**Follow-Up After Sending a Quote:**
Most trades businesses send a quote and wait. 2-3 days later, call with this AI-drafted message: 'Write a brief, non-pushy follow-up text/call script for 3 days after sending a quote for [service]. Remind them of the key value (what problem we solve, not just what we're doing), acknowledge they may be comparing quotes, offer to answer any questions, and give a soft deadline if we have scheduling constraints. Under 60 words for text, or a 30-second phone script.'

customer_communication.title: Customer Communication Scripts
customer_communication.body:
How you communicate before, during, and after a job directly impacts your review score and referral rate. Most trades customers review based on communication quality as much as work quality.

**Job Confirmation Text (send the day before):**
'Hi [Customer Name], confirming your [service] appointment for tomorrow, [date] between [arrival window, e.g., 8-10am]. Our technician will call or text 30 minutes before arrival. If any questions come up, call or text us at [phone]. See you tomorrow!'

**En-Route Notification:**
'Hi [Name], this is [Tech Name] from [Company]. I'm about [X] minutes away. See you shortly!'

**Job Completion Follow-Up (same day):**
'Hi [Name], thanks for having us out today. [Tech Name] completed [brief description of work]. If you notice anything or have any questions in the next few days, call us directly at [phone] — we stand behind our work. Your invoice is attached.'

**Post-Service Review Request (48 hours after):**
'Hi [Name], hope everything with the [service] is working perfectly. If you're happy with the work, a quick Google review would mean a lot to our small team: [link]. Takes under a minute. Thank you!'

**Complaint Response Script:**
'I'm so sorry to hear about the issue. That is absolutely not the standard we hold ourselves to. I want to make this right personally. Can I have [tech/owner] come out to look at this? We will not charge you for a return visit — that's our commitment. What time works for you?'

seasonal_campaigns.title: Seasonal Maintenance Reminder Campaigns
seasonal_campaigns.body:
Seasonal campaigns are the highest-ROI marketing a trades business can run. Your existing customers already trust you and are the most likely to book again. A single seasonal reminder campaign often generates 20-40% of a trades business's monthly revenue.

**Campaign Calendar by Trade:**

HVAC:
- March/April: 'Spring AC tune-up before the heat hits'
- September/October: 'Fall furnace check before the cold'
- December/January: 'Emergency heating service available — have our number handy'

Plumbing:
- October/November: 'Winter pipe protection — winterize before a burst pipe'
- March/April: 'Spring plumbing inspection — catch issues before summer'
- August: 'Summer water heater check — they fail most often in summer heat'

Electrical:
- November/December: 'Holiday lights and electrical load — make sure your panel is ready'
- March: 'Spring electrical safety inspection'
- June: 'Summer AC electrical load — check your circuit capacity'

Landscaping:
- March: 'Spring cleanup and planting — booking spots filling fast'
- October: 'Fall cleanup, mulch, and winterization'
- January: 'Early spring design consultations — limited slots'

**Seasonal Campaign Email Prompt:**
'Write a seasonal email campaign for a [trade] company to send to past customers in [month]. The service angle: [e.g., pre-winter furnace tune-up]. Include: why this timing matters (a specific seasonal risk or benefit), what the service includes, how to book, and a limited availability message if appropriate (only use if true). Tone: helpful and local, not pushy. Under 200 words. Include a strong subject line and a clear call-to-action button label.'

review_generation.title: Review Generation for Trades Businesses
review_generation.body:
Trades businesses depend on Google reviews more than almost any other local business type. When someone's toilet is overflowing at 9pm, they Google 'plumber near me' and call the business with the most recent 5-star reviews. Your review volume and recency is your competitive advantage.

**The In-Person Review Ask:**
At job completion, when the customer has expressed satisfaction (they say something like 'great job' or 'really appreciate it'), say: 'Thank you so much. I don't know if you know this, but for a small local company like ours, Google reviews are how we get the word out. Would you have 30 seconds to leave us one? I can text you the link right now.' Pull out your phone, send the link as a text.

**Automated Post-Job Text:**
Set this up to send automatically 48 hours after an invoice is marked paid: 'Hi [Name], hope the [service] is working great! If you're happy with the work, a Google review would genuinely help our business: [link]. 30 seconds and it makes a real difference for our team. Thank you!'

**What to Ask Them to Write (conversationally):**
'If you do write a review, anything you can say about the specific problem we fixed and how we handled it is really helpful to other homeowners in [city] who might be dealing with the same thing.'

This instruction naturally leads to reviews that mention your service type and city — which are the keywords that help you show up in Google local search.

job_documentation.title: Job Documentation and Warranty Tracking
job_documentation.body:
**Job Documentation Prompt:**
'Create a job documentation form for a [trade] company. For each completed job, the form should capture: customer name and contact info, job site address, service date, technician name, description of problem found, work performed (materials and methods), materials used with part numbers, photos taken (checklist), warranty provided (labor and parts separately), follow-up required (yes/no and description), and customer signature acknowledging completion. Format as a printable field form and as a digital form I can use in Google Forms.'

**Warranty Tracking System:**
'Create a warranty tracking spreadsheet for a service company. Columns: customer name, address, service date, work type, parts warranty expiration, labor warranty expiration, technician, notes, and follow-up date. Add conditional formatting logic (in plain English, since I'll set it up): highlight rows where warranty expiration is within 30 days. Also include a reminder message template I can send when a warranty is about to expire.'

**Warranty Expiration Message:**
'Hi [Name], this is [Company]. We wanted to let you know your warranty on the [service performed] at [address] expires on [date]. If you've noticed any related issues, now is the time to call — we'll cover it at no charge before that date. Everything running well? Just reach out if anything comes up. [Phone]'

lead_generation_content.title: Lead Generation Content: What to Post to Get Local Calls
lead_generation_content.body:
Trades businesses consistently underestimate the power of educational social media content for generating inbound calls. The logic is simple: post a video about a common problem, homeowners with that problem call you.

**Content That Generates Calls (by trade):**

For HVAC:
- 'Your AC is making a rattling noise — here's what it might be (and when to call)'
- '3 signs your furnace is about to fail'
- 'How to change your air filter (and how often you really should)'

For Plumbers:
- 'Why your water pressure suddenly dropped — 3 possible causes'
- 'What that brown water from your tap actually means'
- 'The one thing under your sink you should check every 6 months'

For Electricians:
- 'What a tripping breaker is telling you'
- 'Why your lights flicker (it's not always what you think)'
- '5 electrical safety mistakes homeowners make'

For Landscapers:
- 'Why your lawn has brown patches in summer — and how to fix it'
- 'The 3 plants that survive any [city] winter'
- 'How to tell if your irrigation system is wasting water'

**The Video Formula (shoot on phone, no editing needed):**
Intro (5 seconds): 'If you're a homeowner in [city] and you've ever had [problem], watch this.'
Problem explanation (30-45 seconds): Explain the problem in plain English.
When to DIY vs. call a pro (20 seconds): Be honest. If they can fix it, tell them. They will trust you more and call you for the things they can't.
Call to action (5 seconds): 'If you're dealing with this right now, give us a call at [phone] or visit [website]. We serve [city] and [surrounding areas].'

Post this once per week on Facebook and Instagram. Within 60-90 days, you will start getting calls that reference your videos.

three_immediate_wins.title: 3 Immediate Wins for Trades Business Owners — Do These Today

wins.title: Win 1: Build and Send a Seasonal Reminder Campaign to Past Customers (1 hour)
wins.description: Export your customer list from your invoicing software (QuickBooks, ServiceTitan, Jobber, etc.). Look at the date on the calendar and identify the seasonal campaign that matches your trade (see the seasonal campaign calendar above). Use the campaign email prompt to write the email. Import your customer list to Mailchimp (free). Send the campaign. Most trades businesses generate $3,000-$15,000 in booked work from the first seasonal campaign to their existing customer list. This is the single highest-ROI marketing move a trades business can make.

wins.title: Win 2: Set Up Your Post-Job Review Request Text (30 minutes)
wins.description: If you use invoicing software (Jobber, ServiceTitan, Housecall Pro, QuickBooks), check if it has automated review request messaging built in — most modern field service software does. Turn it on. Write the message using the template above. Get your Google review link and shorten it. Set the trigger to fire 48 hours after invoice is paid or job is marked complete. From this point forward, every completed job automatically requests a review. Trades businesses that do this typically double their review volume within 60 days.

wins.title: Win 3: Film and Post One Educational Video This Week (30 minutes)
wins.description: Pick one common customer problem from the lead generation content list above that matches your trade. Film a 60-90 second video on your phone explaining the problem and when to call a pro. Post it to Facebook and Instagram with a caption that includes your city and phone number. Do not overthink the production quality — authenticity outperforms polish for trades content. Repeat this once per week for 8 weeks and measure how your inbound inquiry volume changes. Most trades businesses see a 20-40% increase in inbound calls from consistent educational video content within 90 days.

quick_start:
You have 97 pages of playbooks, templates, and tools in your hands. Here is how to not get overwhelmed.

**Day 1 (30 minutes):**
Read Section 1 (The AI Audit). Do the 20-task checklist. Count your checkmarks. Highlight the top 3 tasks that are eating the most of your time or directly costing you money (missed leads, no-shows, no reviews).

**Day 2 (1 hour):**
Sign up for ChatGPT Plus ($20/month) at chat.openai.com. Open the playbook section for your specific business type. Read the '3 Immediate Wins' section for your industry. Pick one. Start it today.

**Week 1 Focus:**
Implement the first immediate win completely. Do not move to the second until the first is running.

**Week 2 Focus:**
Implement the second immediate win. Also set up your review request automation (covered in Section 3).

**Week 3-4 Focus:**
Implement the third immediate win. Then read Section 2 (Customer Communication) and identify one additional automation to build.

**Month 2:**
Batch-create one month of social media content using Section 4. Set up your email list if you do not have one.

**Month 3:**
Run your first seasonal or re-engagement campaign to your existing customer database. This is where you start seeing significant revenue impact.

**The only rule:**
Finish before you start the next thing. One completed automation outperforms three half-built ones every time.
about_author:
This playbook was developed from direct implementation work across 40+ local businesses spanning restaurants, salons, gyms, real estate agencies, and trades businesses. The tools, templates, and workflows in this kit are drawn from actual deployments — not theory or case studies from Fortune 500 companies.

Local business is different from enterprise. You don't have a marketing team, an IT department, or a budget for experimentation.
